name: Ginsberg v. New York
summary: The Court upheld a New York law criminalizing the sale of 'girlie magazines' to minors under 17, applying rational basis review and holding that a state may define obscenity differently for children than for adults.
significance: Texas and the Fifth Circuit relied on Ginsberg as the controlling precedent establishing that rational basis review applies to laws restricting minors' access to sexually explicit material; petitioners argued it addressed only the rights of minors, not burdens imposed on adults seeking access.
